title: Sick and carer's leave
content:
Sick and carer's leave

Sick and carer's leave lets an employee take time off to deal with personal illness, caring responsibilities, or family emergencies. Also known as personal leave or personal/carer's leave.

Paid sick leave:
Full-time and part-time employees can take paid sick leave if they can't work because of a personal illness or injury.
- Full-time employees: 10 sick days per year
- Part-time employees: pro-rata entitlement
- Unused sick and carer's leave is carried over to the next year
- Casual employees: NOT entitled to paid sick leave

Paid and unpaid carer's leave:
Employees can take carer's leave (paid or unpaid) if they need to look after an immediate family member or household member who is sick, injured, or affected by an unexpected emergency.

Paid carer's leave:
- Available to full-time and part-time employees
- Comes from the same 10-day entitlement pool as paid sick leave
- Can be taken when a family/household member needs care due to: personal illness, injury, or unexpected emergency

Unpaid carer's leave:
- Casual employees: entitled to 2 days of unpaid carer's leave per occasion
- Full-time and part-time employees: can take unpaid carer's leave if no paid sick/carer's leave remains

Unexpected emergencies:
An unexpected emergency is an unforeseen or sudden and urgent event or situation. Emergencies are not limited to illnesses/injuries and can include things like picking up a child from school.

Immediate family members or household members:
Immediate family member includes:
- spouse or former spouse
- de facto partner or former de facto partner
- child, parent, grandparent, grandchild, sibling
- child, parent, grandparent, grandchild or sibling of the employee's spouse or de facto partner
- Step-relations and adoptive relations included
- A household member is any person who lives with the employee

Notice and medical certificates:
An employee needs to give their employer reasonable evidence that they aren't fit for work if their employer asks for it.

Source: Fair Work Act 2009 sections 12, 95-97, 102-103

key_facts.sick_leave_accrual: Progressive, accumulates year to year if unused
key_facts.paid_personal_leave_entitlement: 10 days per year (for personal illness or caring responsibilities)
